summary: 2026-07-24 11:46:29 PDT — The linter passes all 93 checks, and the dirty-tree, Table B, and fail-closed operational wording is correct. However, two current schema descriptions still contradict the revised skill contract.

findings.title: Canonical schema still makes omissionRisk deterministic
findings.claim: The changed text says “omissionRisk and why are descriptive prose” and “are not part of the byte-reproducibility contract,” but the current canonical plan-schema.json line 146 still says “unitIds/units/runs/estTokens/omissionRisk derive deterministically.” The canonical schema therefore preserves the exact determinism claim this change intended to remove.
findings.suggestion: Update the rowIntent description in plan-schema.json to limit deterministic derivation to unitIds, units, runs, and estTokens, and add a linter pin for that wording or an absence check for the old omissionRisk claim.

findings.title: Schema still attributes uniqueness to the timestamp itself
findings.claim: The changed skill correctly says the one-second timestamp “is not collision-proof on its own” and requires a fail-closed existence check. The current canonical schema at line 23 instead says the timestamp form means “every invocation writes a unique path and can never overwrite” an earlier plan. That is false for same-second invocations: the second invocation must stop without writing, and uniqueness is provided by the guard rather than the filename key.
findings.suggestion: Revise compiledAt's schema description to state that the one-second key may collide and that fresh writes must fail closed when the path exists. Extend the linter to reject the superseded “every invocation writes a unique path” claim.
